text: The concept of 'availability creep' describes how the expectation of responsiveness expands to fill whatever hours a medium can reach. Studies of hospital physicians found that after pager systems replaced fixed-schedule call duties, on-call doctors reported being unable to fully relax even during designated off-hours — their bodies remained in a state of physiological vigilance. The device did not merely extend working hours; it blurred the boundary between on-duty and off-duty at the neurological level, keeping the autonomic nervous system primed for response even when no message had yet arrived.
question: According to the passage, what did research on physicians with pagers reveal about off-hours rest?

options.text: Pager systems reduced physician burnout by replacing unpredictable interruptions with scheduled calls
Doctors remained in physiological vigilance even during designated off-hours, unable to fully relax
Physicians adapted within six months and learned to ignore the pager during leisure time
The study found no measurable difference in cortisol between pager users and non-users
